title: Cassandra racemosa
intro: Cassandra racemosa, also known as Borreria racemosa, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, Cassandra racemosa has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cassandra racemosa is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used in the form of a tincture or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
